evidence: Migration-aware config readers verified on merged main (fe9af165). Gates: npm run build / check / test all exit 0. The first cut of the sdk change was caught by the existing resolve-ref suite as a real safety regression (null-on-unresolvable silently disabled the foreign prefix invariant, letting a mis-filed foreign id resolve 'foreign' instead of degrading 'dangling') and corrected to the two-tier contract before merge — the guarded property held by the suite is preserved. Live parity: context-check-status on the converged active substrate reports all 17 assets in-sync through the promoted operator.

criteria_results.criterion: resolveOperationVocabulary reads via the migration-aware loader: current config -> overrides; unresolvable schema_version -> TOOL_OPERATION_DEFAULTS, never the raw un-migrated shape
criteria_results.evidence: capability-composer.test.ts: new fail-safe test (schema_version 0.0.1 with tool_operations -> exact defaults, override does not surface); existing tests pin pointer-less -> defaults and current-config -> merged overrides

criteria_results.criterion: loadConfigForDirBestEffort two-tier: migrated shape where a chain resolves; raw-parse fallback on unresolvable/invalid preserving the dangling guarantee; null on absent/unparsable
criteria_results.evidence: resolve-ref.test.ts foreign-build-failure suite green (mis-filed foreign id still degrades to dangling via the raw-fallback-fed prefix invariant); structured-endpoints.test.ts new cell: unresolvable foreign config forms the alias endpoint without throwing

criteria_results.criterion: Tests cover both cells at both sites; gates green in dist/
criteria_results.evidence: 8/8 capability-composer, 18/18 structured-endpoints, full suite exit 0; dist rebuilt + operator promoted on merged main
